name: Ladakh
landmark: Pangong Tso, a sapphire-blue high-altitude lake at 4,350m
stopoverNote: Fly into Leh only if you can keep Day 1 completely relaxed for altitude acclimatization. Overland approaches via Srinagar–Leh or Manali–Leh are scenic but seasonal; the Manali road usually opens June–October and the Srinagar road around May–October.

diet.vegetarian: Leh is veg-friendly with Tibetan, North Indian and café menus; veg thukpa, veg momos, dal-rice and sabzi-roti are easy. In Nubra and Pangong, options narrow to guesthouse meals, dal, rice, aloo dishes and veg momos, so request vegetarian food while booking camps or homestays.
diet.vegan: Vegan food is feasible in Leh but limited at altitude. Ask clearly for no milk, butter, paneer or ghee; butter tea is not vegan. Carry nuts, energy bars and dry fruit for the Nubra–Pangong stretch where dairy-heavy camp meals are common.
phrases: Doodh, makhan, paneer, ghee nahi = no milk, butter, paneer or ghee
Sirf sabzi ke momos = vegetable momos only
Black tea dena, butter tea nahi = black tea, not butter tea

cities.name: Leh
cities.notes: Ladakh's high-altitude capital at 3,524m — a Silk Road trading town, monastery base and mandatory acclimatization hub before Nubra or Pangong.

cities.name: Nubra Valley
cities.notes: A wide cold-desert valley around Diskit and Hunder, reached via Khardung La, with Bactrian camels, dunes, monasteries and Balti culture toward Turtuk.

cities.name: Pangong Tso
cities.notes: A 134-km glacial finger lake at 4,350m, famous for turquoise-to-cobalt color shifts, wild skies and severe high-altitude cold nights.

itinerary.sim: Use an Indian postpaid connection; Jio and BSNL postpaid are the most useful in Leh, while prepaid SIMs from outside Jammu & Kashmir/Ladakh may not work. Expect patchy or no signal in Nubra and Pangong; download offline maps and carry printed permits.

Leh.name: Leh
Leh.note: Keep the first 24 hours gentle: flying straight to 3,524m is the highest AMS-risk approach. Use Leh as the base for permits, monastery drives and a final Sham Valley or market half-day.

days.note: CRITICAL: Day 1 must be rest for AMS prevention — no Khardung La, monastery circuits, hikes or altitude gain. Hydrate, eat light, avoid alcohol, and consult a doctor before using Diamox.

activities.name: Hotel rest and acclimatization in Leh
activities.tip: Stay horizontal for the first 4–6 hours after landing; book a central hotel or guesthouse that can arrange oxygen if needed.

activities.name: Leh Main Bazaar short flat walk
activities.tip: Only if you feel well after 4pm; keep it flat and brief.

activities.name: Shanti Stupa viewpoint by car
activities.tip: Drive up for sunset views; do not climb the steps on Day 1.

activities.name: Light dinner at Gesmo or The Tibetan Kitchen
activities.tip: Choose thukpa, rice or veg momos; avoid alcohol and heavy meals.

hotels.name: Leh family guesthouse near Main Bazaar
The Grand Dragon / Stok Palace Heritage

meals: Veg thukpa and momos at Gesmo
Light rice bowl at The Tibetan Kitchen
Plain black tea instead of butter tea

days.theme: Indus Valley monasteries and Leh heritage
days.note: Stay around Leh's altitude on Day 2. If headache or nausea persists, cut the circuit short and rest instead of pushing through the full monastery run.

activities.name: Thiksey Monastery rooftop and assembly hall
activities.tip: The 6:30am puja is memorable but optional; avoid rushing if still acclimatizing.

activities.name: Hemis Monastery and museum
activities.tip: Ladakh's largest monastery; ask locally about Hemis Festival dates.

activities.name: Hall of Fame Museum or Leh Palace

hotels.name: Leh family guesthouse near Main Bazaar

days.note: Use this as the final Leh half-day after returning from Pangong if your flight timing allows; otherwise keep it as a relaxed market-and-rest buffer.

activities.name: Alchi Monastery frescoes in Sham Valley
activities.tip: The 11th-century Indo-Kashmiri murals are unique in Ladakh; leave Leh early for the 67km drive.

activities.name: Leh Main Bazaar for pashmina, apricot products and prayer flags
activities.tip: Withdraw cash in Leh before leaving for Nubra/Pangong; ATMs outside Leh are unreliable.

activities.name: Café stop at Bon Appetit or Lehvenda Café

hotels.name: Leh family guesthouse near Main Bazaar

meals: Lehvenda Café coffee and apple pie

Nubra Valley.name: Nubra Valley
Nubra Valley.note: Two days lets you cross Khardung La safely, see Diskit and Hunder at a lower altitude than Leh, and add Turtuk or Sumur/Panamik without making every stop feel rushed.

days.theme: Khardung La, Diskit Buddha and Hunder dunes
days.note: Start Leh by 7–8am. Stop at Khardung La only briefly — it is above 5,300m and thin-air symptoms can appear fast.

activities.name: Khardung La pass photo stop
activities.tip: Walk slowly, take photos quickly, and descend toward Nubra rather than lingering at the pass.

activities.name: Diskit Monastery and 32m Maitreya Buddha
activities.tip: The monastery dates to 1420 and has wide Shyok Valley views.

activities.name: Hunder Sand Dunes and Bactrian camel ride
activities.tip: Golden hour is best; choose short rides and treat animals responsibly.

days.theme: Turtuk's Balti culture or Sumur hot-spring loop
days.note: Turtuk is a long but rewarding day trip from Hunder. If roads or permits are uncertain, switch to a relaxed Sumur–Panamik loop instead.

activities.name: Turtuk village and Balti heritage walk
activities.tip: Start by 8am; a local guide for ₹200–₹500 adds context on Balti homes, orchards and border history.

activities.name: Panamik Hot Springs
activities.tip: Use this as the gentler alternative if skipping Turtuk.

meals: Homestay thali requested vegetarian in advance
Carry snacks for the Turtuk road

Pangong Tso.name: Pangong Tso
Pangong Tso.note: Stay one night to see sunset, stars and sunrise, but treat the 4,350m altitude seriously. Camps are seasonal and shoreline camping is not allowed; sleep in village-boundary camps or cottages.

days.theme: Shyok road arrival, blue lake and sunset
days.note: Drive from Nubra early so Shyok water crossings are done before afternoon snowmelt rises. Pangong is higher than Leh; avoid strenuous walking on arrival.

activities.name: Pangong Tso lakeside walk and photography
activities.tip: Walk slowly near Spangmik; the color shifts from turquoise to cobalt through the afternoon.

activities.name: Sunset at Spangmik shore
activities.tip: September often gives calm reflections and clear skies.

activities.name: Bar-headed geese, Brahminee ducks and marmot spotting
activities.tip: Bring binoculars and keep distance from nesting birds.

activities.name: Stargazing from camp
activities.tip: Temperatures can drop to 2–5°C even in summer; layer up after dinner.

meals: Aloo sabzi and roti requested vegetarian

days.theme: Pangong sunrise and Chang La return to Leh
days.note: Leave after sunrise for the 5–6 hour drive back to Leh via Chang La. The pass is around 5,360m, so keep stops short and warm.

activities.name: Pangong sunrise before departure
activities.tip: Set a 5:30am alarm; dawn light is the payoff for the overnight stay.

activities.name: Chang La pass photo stop
activities.tip: Do not run or climb; get back in the vehicle quickly if breathless.

activities.name: Hemis Monastery en route if missed earlier

connections.method: Private SUV/Innova via North Pullu, Khardung La, Khalsar and Diskit
connections.cost: ₹9,744–₹11,807 per vehicle; part of ₹22,410 3-day Nubra–Pangong circuit

connections.method: Private SUV via Khalsar, Shyok Valley Road, Agham, Tangtse and Spangmik
connections.cost: Included in ₹22,410 3-day circuit; standalone leg often ₹12,000–₹14,000

connections.method: Private SUV via Tangtse, Chang La and Karu
connections.cost: Included in ₹22,410 circuit; standalone ₹10,470–₹12,464 per vehicle

extras: Inner Line / Protected Area permit formalities are mandatory for Nubra and Pangong. Indian citizens pay the online environmental/conservation fees: ₹400 environmental fee + ₹100 Red Cross fee + ₹20 wildlife fee per person per day, about ₹520–₹660 for a 7-day trip; foreign nationals need PAP through a registered Leh agent.
Carry 5–6 printed permit copies for North Pullu, South Pullu, Tangtse, Spangmik and other army/police checkposts; mobile signal can be absent when you need to show paperwork.

Roads to Nubra and Pangong are reliable only around May–October, with the best operating window June–September. November–April brings extreme cold, high-pass closures and closed camps; May and October can still see snow-related disruptions.

The standard 3-day Leh→Nubra→Pangong→Leh private SUV circuit is about ₹22,410 per vehicle in the 2025–26 Ladakh Taxi Union season; costs are per vehicle and become much cheaper per person for groups of 4–6.
Direct shoreline camping at Pangong is banned; use camps/cottages set back in village boundaries and do not drive onto fragile lake edges.
Vegetarian meals are workable everywhere, but vegan and varied veg options are limited at altitude outside Leh. Book meals in advance for Nubra/Pangong and carry dry fruit, bars and cash.

Acute Mountain Sickness is the main feasibility constraint: Day 1 in Leh must be relaxed, high-pass stops at Khardung La and Chang La should be 15–20 minutes max, and worsening symptoms require descent and medical help.

Flights from Delhi to Leh typically run about ₹4,700–₹9,720 one-way when booked ahead, but peak July–August or last-minute fares can rise to ₹10,000–₹17,000. Most overall budgets above include return flights from Delhi.
